mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over two months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over two months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, boosting corporate profit margins and equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production signal immediate supply chain bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth rates do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than chance (win rate < 45%).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, high borrowing costs compress equity valuations and force portfolio rebalancing away from growth stocks, producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in > 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real GDP growth reflect stronger macroeconomic momentum, which immediately improves market sentiment and corporate earnings forecasts, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show no predictive power for 5-day forward returns (t-statistic < 1.5 in out-of-sample tests).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth drives positive equity returns only when inflation remains subdued, as low price pressures preserve real corporate margins and investor risk appetite.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL is below 230.0 (win rate < 40%).
